title: INC001-RANSOMWARE-202511717
tasks: [{"createdBy": "orgadmin0001@ir-org.com", "createdAt": "17/11/2025 18:15", "title": "Containment", "description": "Isolate ws-user01 and ws-user02; this is where the dropper binary was executed (we think) Isolate backup server (srv-backup01.irorg.local) to protect data  Rotate credentials on dc01.irorg.local  Disconnect file server from all networks and power off (srv-web01.irorg.local)  Disconnect machines which are not affected  Block 185.73.125.8 and 94.232.46.202 and 69.4.234.20 185.59.221.75 to reduce the likelihood that attackers will be able to connect to the system.", "owner": "analyst00001@ir-org.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "17/11/2025 19:15", "endDate": "17/11/2025 19:15"}, {"createdBy": "orgadmin0001@ir-org.com", "createdAt": "17/11/2025 17:58", "title": "Detect", "description": "Within minutes, employees begin reporting that their departmental shared folders are “corrupted,” “empty,” or “full of files with strange extensions.\" That extension is: .df_locked. A text file appears in every encrypted directory, formatted as a ransom note. The note identifies a threat actor known for using a LockBit 3.0-based ransomware builder. This combination of activities matches several patterns in CIRCL threat intelligence reports.", "owner": "analyst00001@ir-org.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "17/11/2025 19:15", "endDate": "17/11/2025 19:15"}, {"createdBy": "orgadmin0001@ir-org.com", "createdAt": "17/11/2025 18:37", "title": "Eradication", "description": "Delete exe files from impacted machines  Rotate AD admin credentials a second time. (reset krbtgt account password twice)", "owner": "analyst00001@ir-org.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "17/11/2025 19:15", "endDate": "17/11/2025 19:15"}, {"createdBy": "orgadmin0001@ir-org.com", "createdAt": "17/11/2025 18:14", "title": "Analysis", "description": "", "owner": "analyst00001@ir-org.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "17/11/2025 19:15", "endDate": "17/11/2025 19:15"}, {"createdBy": "orgadmin0001@ir-org.com", "createdAt": "17/11/2025 18:43", "title": "Recovery", "description": "* Back up AD Domain Controller to separate machine then isolate. * Create new AD Domain COntroller, then restore from backup; observe new machine for signs of compromise, e.g. users logging in from abroad) Recreate base file server image.  Test backup server (srv-backup01.irorg.lo cal) on an isolated network. * If the backup server works, restore file server.", "owner": "analyst00001@ir-org.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "17/11/2025 19:15", "endDate": "17/11/2025 19:15"}, {"createdBy": "orgadmin0001@ir-org.com", "createdAt": "17/11/2025 18:43", "title": "Post-Incident Activity", "description": "Lessons learned:  1. Attacker logged in with RDP and executed a dropper binary - Are there User Access Reviews? How did this start? DId they click on a phishing link? 2. Powershell script run on user machine-why are we allowing this? 3. User was able to install executables - Do they have excess permissions? 4. Windows event log was cleared - Is it being backed up? 5. Encryption module attempted to run on the backup server - What is our backup process? Should we have other backups to the cloud? Who has and who needs access to the backup server? 6. How well prepared were we? What do we need to do in terms of creating a new incident response plan? How often will we need to test it? 7. Did we have EDR? Was it working properly? 8. What's our business continuity plan? 9. Do we have cyber insurance? 10. Was the data exfiltrated? Does the Data Protection Commissioner need to be notified? 11. Data Protection Officer needs to be notified for a Data Protection Impact Assessment. 12. Is the file server being actively monitored by XDR?", "owner": "analyst00001@ir-org.com", "status": "Completed", "startDate": "17/11/2025 19:15", "endDate": "17/11/2025 19:15"}]
